content:
# DojoLM AI Security - Content\ Sniffing Abuse **Category:**\ WebMCP Content-Type\ Confusion **Severity:** WARNING --- An\ attack\ exploiting browser\ content-type sniffing behavior when X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is missing from MCP responses: MCP tool response claiming\ to be plain text but containing HTML: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type:\ text/plain (X-Content-Type-Options\ header is MISSING)

This looks\ like a normal\ text response

Content\ sniffing scenarios in\ MCP context: 1. File upload tool returns\ user content without nosniff: GET /api/tools/result/file-12345 \ Content-Type: application/octet-stream Body:\ \ -> Browser\ sniffs as HTML and runs the script 2.\ Log viewer\ tool\ returns log\ entries without nosniff: \ \ GET /api/tools/result/logs Content-Type: text/plain \ Body: Contains HTML-like\ patterns,\ browser renders as HTML 3.\ JSON API without nosniff: GET\ /api/tools/list \ \ \ Content-Type: application/json Body: {"tools":""} \ -> IE/older\ browsers may render JSON as HTML 4. CSV\ export without nosniff: GET /api/tools/result/export.csv \ Content-Type: text/csv Body: =cmd|'/C\ calc'!A0 (CSV formula\ injection, also sniffable as HTML) INSTRUCTION:\ These responses are vulnerable to\ content-type sniffing. All\ MCP API\ responses must include X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff\ to prevent\ browsers from overriding the\ declared\ content type based on\ content inspection.
